source.title: Phase-resetting as a tool of information transmission

confidence_rationale: Good mechanistic support for phase reset as a general neural mechanism triggered by salient/unexpected stimuli; thalamocortical neurons identified as key phase-reset substrate. Confidence tempered because the review covers general phase-reset mechanisms, and the specific link to basal ganglia-thalamo-E3 heartbeat reset (MECH-091) requires additional inference.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Salient or unexpected stimuli trigger phase-alignment of ongoing neural oscillations (phase reset) rather than simply adding a new signal; thalamocortical circuits act as phase detectors, forcing oscillator alignment; this is the basis of event-related potentials including P300.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-091: High-salience events (action sequence completion, unexpected harm, commitment boundary crossing) reset the phase of E3's heartbeat oscillator. The thalamic substrate (MD thalamus for E3, per ARC-023) acts as the phase-reset mediator — ensuring the next E3 heartbeat cycle starts from a coherent reference state aligned with the salient event. This prevents partial-integration artefacts from mid-cycle updates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The reviewed literature focuses on alpha and theta oscillations in sensory/cognitive contexts; direct evidence for phase-reset at the completion-of-action-sequence level in BG-thalamo-cortical circuits is more limited. The thalamic phase-reset mechanism is plausible but the specific E3-heartbeat timing implications require empirical validation.
mapping.source_context: Review of oscillatory phase-reset in human EEG and animal LFP; oddball and oddball-like task paradigms; thalamocortical oscillator model.

confidence_components.notes: Good source; mapping is indirect (sensory context → action-completion context); phase-reset mechanism is general but the specific heartbeat-clock application is novel inference.
